questions.question: Explain the three depth-first traversals of a binary tree with an example tree showing visit order.
keyPoints: Define Preorder: Root → Left → Right
Define Inorder: Left → Root → Right
Define Postorder: Left → Right → Root
Give example tree and show traversal sequences

questions.question: Write an algorithm for iterative Inorder traversal using a stack.
keyPoints: Initialize empty stack
Traverse to leftmost node pushing nodes
Pop and process node
Move to right subtree
Repeat until stack empty and current is null

questions.question: Explain Level Order traversal with its algorithm and applications.
keyPoints: Level order visits level by level
Uses queue data structure
Algorithm: Enqueue root, then repeatedly dequeue and enqueue children
Applications: Finding shortest path, level-width problems

questions.question: What is an expression tree? How is it evaluated using traversal? Give an example.
keyPoints: Expression tree: operators at internal nodes, operands at leaves
Postfix evaluation: recursively evaluate left, right, then apply operator
Example: (a+b)*c → tree with * at root, + at left, a,b at left leaves, c at right leaf
Result from postorder traversal gives correct evaluation

questions.question: Compare time and space complexity of recursive tree traversals.
keyPoints: Time complexity: O(n) - each node visited once
Space complexity: O(h) where h = height of tree
Best case (balanced): O(log n)
Worst case (skewed): O(n)
Space used by call stack in recursion

questions.question: Explain why postorder traversal is used for deleting a tree.
keyPoints: Postorder visits children before parent
Must free child memory before parent
If parent deleted first, children become inaccessible (memory leak)
Process: delete left subtree, delete right subtree, delete root node

questions.question: Differentiate between Depth-First Search and Breadth-First Search in tree traversal.
keyPoints: DFS goes deep before wide (uses stack/recursion)
BFS goes wide before deep (uses queue)
DFS traversals: Preorder, Inorder, Postorder
BFS traversal: Level Order
DFS uses O(h) space, BFS uses O(w) space

questions.question: For a binary tree, write recursive algorithms for all three depth-first traversals (Preorder, Inorder, Postorder) and trace them on the following tree: Root A has left child B and right child C; B has left child D and right child E; C has left child F.
keyPoints: Write recursive Preorder: Visit(root), traverse(left), traverse(right)
Write recursive Inorder: traverse(left), Visit(root), traverse(right)
Write recursive Postorder: traverse(left), traverse(right), Visit(root)
Trace Preorder: A B D E C F
Trace Inorder: D B E A F C
Trace Postorder: D E B F C A
Explain each step of tracing

questions.question: Construct an expression tree for the infix expression (a+b)*(c-d) and show how postfix and prefix traversals give postfix and prefix notations. Also explain evaluation.
keyPoints: Parse expression: + at left of *, - at left of *
Build tree: * at root, + at left child, - at right child
Leaves: a, b under +; c, d under -
Postorder traversal: ab+cd-* (Postfix)
Preorder traversal: *+ab-cd (Prefix)
Evaluation: Use postorder - evaluate ab+ = a+b, cd- = c-d, then multiply results
Explain recursive evaluation process

questions.question: Write complete C programs for (a) Inorder traversal using recursion (b) Level order traversal using queue. Include structure definition and main function with sample tree creation.
keyPoints: For (a): Define tree node structure with data, left, right pointers
Recursive Inorder function: if node exists, call left, print, call right
Time O(n), Space O(h)
For (b): Define queue structure with array and front/rear
Level order: Initialize queue, enqueue root
While queue not empty: dequeue, print, enqueue left if exists, enqueue right if exists
Time O(n), Space O(w) max width

questions.question: A binary search tree contains integers. Write an algorithm to find: (a) Maximum element (b) Minimum element. Explain using traversal concepts.
keyPoints: Maximum: Rightmost node in tree (traverse right until null)
Can use iterative approach or modified inorder
Minimum: Leftmost node in tree (traverse left until null)
For BST: Maximum is last node in inorder (rightmost)
Minimum is first node in inorder (leftmost)
Explain why inorder gives sorted sequence
Provide pseudo-code for both approaches
